response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack/web engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the role requires native-level Japanese, deep C/UNIX systems work, Linux kernel modification, and embedded networking/security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, switch chips). None of these core requirements are evidenced in the resume — C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago, and there is no Japanese proficiency or embedded networking background.
strengths: 10+ years professional software development experience across diverse stacks
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed (hard requirement is native-level)
No Linux kernel modification, embedded firmware, or switch/networking security experience
C is not a current core skill; primary stack is TypeScript/Node/DLang, not UNIX C systems work

key_factors.factor: Insufficient current C/UNIX systems programming depth
